title: penstemon heterodoxus subsp. typicus
intro: Penstemon heterodoxus subsp. typicus, also known as the 'Typical Bastard Beardtongue', is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's extracts have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, making it a potential remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Penstemon heterodoxus subsp. typicus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
